seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI chatbots with OpenAI's Assistants API enables powerful conversational agents with built-in tools like code interpreter, file search, and function calling. However, the autonomous nature of these agents introduces risks like unsafe code execution, hallucinated function calls, and context overflow that can lead to poor user experiences or security vulnerabilities. Vex provides essential guardrails through runtime verification, ensuring response accuracy, safety, and coherence in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
The typical AI chatbot architecture using OpenAI Assistants API centers around creating persistent assistant instances configured with custom instructions, tools (code interpreter, file search, function calling), and vector stores for RAG. User messages initiate threads that maintain multi-turn conversation state, with the API handling tool execution and response generation automatically. Intent classification and memory management occur via function calling to external services or embedded in assistant instructions.

Client applications poll or use webhooks for run completion events, processing responses while managing thread length to prevent context window exhaustion. Retrieval-augmented generation pulls relevant documents from uploaded files, while safety checks and PII filtering require additional verification layers. This managed runtime simplifies deployment but demands robust monitoring for the failure modes inherent to autonomous agent execution.

Vex integrates seamlessly via webhooks on run completion, verifying outputs against safety, accuracy, and coherence criteria before delivery to users.

risks.name: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
risks.description: Assistant may generate and execute dangerous code like file deletions or network requests despite sandboxing
risks.mitigation: Vex safety filtering blocks malicious code patterns and verifies sandbox execution logs

risks.name: File search retrieving irrelevant or outdated documents
risks.description: RAG retrieval returns outdated or off-topic files, causing hallucinated or inaccurate responses
risks.mitigation: Vex response accuracy checks semantic relevance of retrieved content to user query

risks.name: Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
risks.description: Long conversations exceed context window without truncation, leading to incoherent responses
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors thread token usage and enforces conversation summarization triggers

risks.name: Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
risks.description: Assistant generates invalid JSON parameters for tool calls, causing runtime failures
risks.mitigation: Vex validates function call arguments against JSON schemas before execution

implementation.title: Create Thread and Add User Message
implementation.description: Manage conversation state with threads and poll for run completion
implementation.code:
thread = client.beta.threads.create()

message = client.beta.threads.messages.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    role="user",
    content="What's the latest on our Q3 sales?"
)

run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id=assistant.id
)

# Poll for completion
while run.status in ['queued', 'in_progress']:
    run = client.beta.threads.runs.retrieve(
        thread_id=thread.id,
        run_id=run.id
    )
    time.sleep(1)

messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread.id)

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Webhook for Run Completion
vexIntegration.description: Set up Flask webhook endpoint that receives OpenAI run completion events and verifies with Vex
vexIntegration.code:
from flask import Flask, request
pip install openai vex-sdk flask

from vex import Vex
from openai import OpenAI

app = Flask(__name__)
vex = Vex(api_key="vex-your-key")
openai_client = OpenAI()

@app.route('/webhook/openai-run', methods=['POST'])
def vex_webhook():
    event = request.json
    
    if event['event'] == 'thread.run.completed':
        thread_id = event['data']['object']['thread_id']
        run_id = event['data']['object']['id']
        
        # Fetch messages
        messages = openai_client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread_id)
        user_query = messages.data[0].content[0].text.value
        assistant_response = messages.data[0].content[0].text.value
        
        # Vex verification
        result = vex.verify({
            'prompt': user_query,
            'response': assistant_response,
            'verifications': ['response_accuracy', 'safety_filtering', 'pii_protection', 'hallucination_detection']
        })
        
        if not result.approved:
            # Block unsafe response
            return {'status': 'blocked', 'issues': result.issues}, 403
    
    return {'status': 'ok'}

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(port=5000)
